Wade buys a sandwich to eat now, one for the road, and one for in the evening. He also buys 2 drinks. The drinks cost $4 each, so the total cost for the drinks is [drink_cost] * 2 = [total_drink_cost]. Wade spends a total of $26, so the cost of the sandwiches is [total_spent] - [total_drink_cost] = [sandwich_cost]. Since he buys 3 sandwiches, the cost of each sandwich is [sandwich_cost] / 3 = [price_per_sandwich].

The total spent by Wade is $26, which includes the cost of the drinks and the sandwiches. The drinks cost $4 each, so the total cost for the drinks is [drink_cost] * 2 = [total_drink_cost]. The remaining amount, [total_spent] - [total_drink_cost], is spent on the sandwiches. Since he buys 3 sandwiches, the cost of each sandwich is [sandwich_cost] / 3 = [price_per_sandwich]. Lab [address] [price_per_sandwich] [total_spent] [total_drink_cost].
